stimulus.content: Jake has saved ten dollars. He wants to buy a book for ten dollars. He also wants to buy a model airplane for ten dollars. He can only afford one item, so he chooses the book.

stem: In this example, what is Jake's opportunity cost of buying the book?

choices.text: The ten dollars he spent on the book
The model airplane he gave up
The time he spent saving his money
The store where he bought the book

answer_explanation: Opportunity cost is the value of the next best option you give up when you make a choice. Jake chose the book, so his opportunity cost is the model airplane he could not buy. Opportunity cost helps explain the trade-offs people face in economic decisions.
